title: How Long Does Potty Training Take? (2026) – Realistic Timelines by Breed
seoTitle: How Long Does Potty Training Take? Timelines by Breed | GetPawsy
seoDescription: Realistic potty training timelines for puppies and adult dogs. How breed size, consistency, and method affect duration. Expert data.
h1Override: How Long Does Potty Training Take? Realistic Timelines by Breed
excerpt: Realistic potty training duration data organized by breed size, age, and method. Why some dogs take 2 weeks and others take 6.
quickAnswer: Average potty training takes 2–4 weeks for medium/large breed puppies, 4–6 weeks for small/toy breeds, and 1–2 weeks for adult dogs. Consistency is the biggest factor — dogs with strict schedules train 40% faster than those with inconsistent routines.

faq.question: Why is my puppy taking so long to potty train?
faq.answer: The top 3 reasons: inconsistent schedule, too much unsupervised freedom too early, and inadequate cleanup (not using enzyme cleaner). Small breeds legitimately take longer due to smaller bladders — this isn't a training failure.

sections.heading: Timeline by Breed Size
sections.content:
**Large Breeds (Labs, Goldens, Shepherds):** 10–14 days. Larger bladders mean longer hold times and fewer accidents during training.

**Medium Breeds (Beagles, Bulldogs, Corgis):** 2–3 weeks. Standard timeline with consistent scheduling.

**Small Breeds (Yorkies, Chihuahuas, Pomeranians):** 4–6 weeks. Smaller bladders, faster metabolisms, and more hiding spots for accidents.

**Adult Rescue Dogs:** 5–10 days. Adult dogs have bladder control — they just need to learn YOUR routine.

For the fastest method, use our [7-day accelerated guide →](/guides/how-to-potty-train-a-puppy-fast). For essential supplies, browse [potty training gear →](/collections/dog-potty-training).

sections.heading: What Slows Down Training
sections.content:
**Inconsistency:** The #1 factor. If the schedule changes daily, the dog can't form a pattern. Same times, same door, same spot, same reward.

**Free-Feeding:** If food is always available, elimination timing becomes unpredictable. Switch to 2–3 scheduled meals.

**Inadequate Cleanup:** Regular cleaners leave scent markers. Only enzyme cleaners fully break down urine proteins. Browse [enzyme cleaners →](/collections/dog-potty-training).

**Too Much Freedom Too Soon:** Puppies earning supervised room access should have proven 3+ consecutive accident-free days first.

For the complete troubleshooting system, read our [Dog Potty Training Complete Guide →](/guides/dog-potty-training-complete-guide).
